attacking_style: Not an attacker; pure sprinter who occasionally shows exceptional climbing resilience to survive medium-length climbs (up to 2km) to contest technical sprint finishes. Rarely initiates moves.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on domestique lead-outs but increasingly positions himself independently in final 500m. Teams consistently deliver ideal positioning, but conversion rate declining. Domestique effort often wasted when climbs appear.
terrain_preference: Technical uphill sprint finishes (Matrah Corniche specialty), cobblestones, and headwind sprints where positioning matters more than raw power. Vulnerable when climbs exceed 2km or multiple categorized ascents present.
vulnerability: Eliminated on any stage with sustained climbing or multiple categorized climbs. Declining top-end speed since 2020 means perfect positioning no longer guarantees wins. Poor bike-throw technique costs close finishes. Fatigue-related crashes in stage race final stages.
key_patterns: Dominates specific technical finishes through venue experience; three Matrah Corniche wins demonstrate course-learning advantage.
Dropped consistently on climbs longer than 2km or stages with multiple categorized ascents (2019-2022 pattern).
Final 200m speed declining since 2021; perfect positioning no longer converts despite quality lead-outs.
Shows exceptional mid-race recovery capacity; regained contact after distancing to still win sprints (2019 UAE).
Bike-throw technique weakness identified costing stages at Worlds and Tour 2018 despite equal positioning.
Increasingly aggressive/desperate late-sprint tactics including dangerous barrier squeezes when form uncertain (2022, 2017).
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline in top-end speed from 2021 onwards. Despite ideal positioning from UNO-X in 2023, lacks final acceleration. Eliminated earlier on moderate climbs compared to 2017-2019 baseline.
